code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test21-APR-2017Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria and glucosuria, suggesting possible glomerular damage or diabetic nephropathy. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity, and contains bilirubin and ketones. No evidence of urinary tract infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for diabetes and liver involvement.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
